terms.definition: A precision rotary instrument used to drive burs for cutting tooth structure or polishing.
terms.exampleSentence: A high-speed dental handpiece with water spray was used to remove the carious dentine.
terms.contextNotes: Use 'drill' when speaking with patients.

terms.definition: A powered instrument that uses high-frequency vibrations to remove calculus and biofilm from teeth.
terms.exampleSentence: Subgingival calculus was removed with an ultrasonic scaler under copious irrigation.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid in patients with non-shielded pacemakers.

terms.definition: A thin sheet of latex or non-latex material used to isolate one or more teeth during dental procedures.
terms.exampleSentence: A rubber dam was placed to ensure dry isolation during the root canal procedure.
terms.contextNotes: Standard of care for endodontic treatment.

terms.definition: A mechanical device that simulates jaw movements and is used to mount dental casts for prosthodontic work.
terms.exampleSentence: Casts were mounted on a semi-adjustable articulator using a facebow record.
terms.contextNotes: Critical for accurate occlusal records in indirect restorations.

terms.definition: An intraoral X-ray showing the crowns of the upper and lower posterior teeth on a single film.
terms.exampleSentence: Bitewing radiographs were taken to assess for interproximal caries.
terms.contextNotes: Most useful view for detecting interproximal caries.

terms.definition: An intraoral X-ray showing the entire tooth from crown to apex including periapical bone.
terms.exampleSentence: A periapical radiograph confirmed periapical radiolucency around the upper canine.
terms.contextNotes: Essential for endodontic diagnosis and follow-up.

terms.definition: An orthopantomogram: a panoramic dental radiograph showing the maxilla, mandible, and surrounding structures.
terms.exampleSentence: The OPG demonstrated bilateral impacted lower third molars.
terms.contextNotes: Single film captures full dentition and surrounding bone.

terms.definition: Cone-beam computed tomography: a 3D imaging modality used in dentistry for hard-tissue assessment.
terms.exampleSentence: A small-volume CBCT was requested for implant planning in the upper anterior region.
terms.contextNotes: Lower radiation dose than medical CT but justify use.

terms.definition: An amide local anaesthetic widely used in dentistry, often combined with adrenaline for vasoconstriction.
terms.exampleSentence: An inferior alveolar nerve block was performed with 2% lidocaine and 1:80,000 adrenaline.
terms.contextNotes: Maximum dose 4.4 mg/kg with adrenaline (adult).

terms.definition: A short-acting amide local anaesthetic with high tissue penetration, commonly used for infiltration.
terms.exampleSentence: Buccal infiltration with 4% articaine and 1:100,000 adrenaline achieved adequate anaesthesia.
terms.contextNotes: Effective for mandibular infiltration in adults.

terms.definition: Reversible loss of sensation in a defined area produced by application of a local anaesthetic agent.
terms.exampleSentence: The extraction was completed under local anaesthesia without complication.
terms.contextNotes: Always confirm anaesthesia before commencing operative work.

terms.definition: Voluntary agreement to a procedure after the patient has been given full information on risks, benefits, and alternatives.
terms.exampleSentence: Written informed consent was obtained prior to the surgical extraction.
terms.contextNotes: Document discussion of risks and alternatives.

terms.definition: Verbal and written guidance given to a patient following a dental procedure to support healing.
terms.exampleSentence: Post-operative instructions including bite-pressure and analgesia advice were given to the patient.
terms.contextNotes: Provide written copy and confirm understanding.

terms.definition: Personalised guidance on plaque control techniques such as brushing, flossing, and interdental cleaning.
terms.exampleSentence: Tailored oral hygiene advice was provided, including modified Bass brushing technique.
terms.contextNotes: OHI underpins all periodontal therapy.

terms.definition: Instructions on cleaning, soaking, and storing removable dentures to maintain oral health.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient was given written denture care advice and shown how to brush the denture daily.
terms.contextNotes: Recommend leaving dentures out overnight.

terms.definition: Brief intervention encouraging and supporting patients to stop smoking, with referral to cessation services.
terms.exampleSentence: Smoking cessation advice was given and referral to the local stop-smoking service was made.
terms.contextNotes: Use the NHS Very Brief Advice (VBA) framework.

terms.definition: Guidance on food and drink choices to reduce caries risk, focusing on sugar frequency and acid intake.
terms.exampleSentence: Dietary advice focused on reducing the frequency of sugary snacks between meals.
terms.contextNotes: Use a 3-day diet diary to identify risk factors.

terms.definition: A written communication from one clinician to another requesting specialist assessment or treatment.
terms.exampleSentence: A referral letter was sent to the oral and maxillofacial surgeon for biopsy.
terms.contextNotes: Core writing task in the OET dentistry exam.

terms.definition: A structured record of a patient's past and current medical conditions, medications, and allergies.
terms.exampleSentence: An updated medical history was taken before commencing treatment.
terms.contextNotes: Verify at every recall appointment.

terms.definition: Fluoride applied directly to the tooth surface to enhance enamel resistance to acid attack.
terms.exampleSentence: Topical fluoride toothpaste of 1450 ppm was prescribed for daily use.
terms.contextNotes: 5000 ppm fluoride toothpaste is prescription-only.

terms.definition: An antiseptic oral rinse used short-term to control plaque and treat gingival inflammation.
terms.exampleSentence: Chlorhexidine 0.2% mouthwash was prescribed twice daily for two weeks.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid long-term use due to staining and taste effects.

terms.definition: A broad-spectrum penicillin antibiotic commonly used in dentistry for spreading odontogenic infection.
terms.exampleSentence: Oral amoxicillin 500 mg three times daily was prescribed for the spreading dental infection.
terms.contextNotes: Adjunct only — drainage of pus is essential.

terms.definition: An antibiotic active against anaerobic bacteria, used for periodontal and odontogenic infections.
terms.exampleSentence: Metronidazole 400 mg three times daily was added to amoxicillin for the periodontal abscess.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel patient to avoid alcohol during course.

terms.definition: A medication that reduces blood clotting, relevant in dentistry due to bleeding risk during surgery.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient's anticoagulant therapy was reviewed prior to the planned extraction.
terms.contextNotes: Follow SDCEP guidance for management around surgery.

terms.definition: A scheduled review visit to assess healing, treatment outcomes, or disease progression.
terms.exampleSentence: A follow-up appointment was arranged in two weeks to remove sutures and assess healing.
terms.contextNotes: Document outcome and next interval clearly.
